seoTitle: Convert a Word Document to Google Docs Easily
metaDescription: Upload a .docx file to Google Drive and convert it to an editable Google Doc, plus how to auto-convert future uploads and preserve formatting.
intro: Moving away from desktop Word files to cloud collaboration starts with converting your existing .docx documents. This guide covers uploading a Word file to Drive, converting it into a native Google Doc, and setting up automatic conversion for future uploads.

steps.heading: Upload your Word file to Drive
steps.body: Use the upload wizard in Google Drive to add your .docx file to your cloud storage.

steps.heading: Understand the two viewing modes
steps.body: Know the difference between viewing a Word file in Office Editing mode and fully converting it into a Google Doc.

steps.heading: Convert with Save as Google Docs
steps.body: Open the file and use the File menu's Save as Google Docs option to run the conversion.

steps.heading: Turn on automatic conversion
steps.body: In your Drive settings, enable automatic conversion so future uploaded Office files convert to Google Workspace formats by default.

steps.heading: Check formatting after conversion
steps.body: Review fonts, tables, and alignment to catch any structural shifts before sharing the document.

faq.question: Will my Word formatting survive the conversion?
faq.answer: Most formatting carries over, but it's worth checking fonts, tables, and alignment afterward since some structural shifting can occur.

faq.question: Can I make Drive convert every uploaded Word file automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, there's a setting in Google Drive preferences to automatically convert future uploaded Office files into Workspace formats.

faq.question: What's the difference between viewing and converting a Word file?
faq.answer: Office Editing mode lets you view and lightly edit the file in its original format, while converting creates a fully native Google Doc.

faq.question: Can I still export back to Word after converting?
faq.answer: Yes, once converted, you can share or export the document back to Word format while maintaining version control.
